Here's the breakdown of the technical content, organized according to your request:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**
*   No explicit mathematical equations are present in the text.

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 2: Representative stability map of the combustor with combustor length for Baseline 0% H2 and 40% H2**
    *   **Axes:**
        *   Y-axis (left):  P'RMS / Patm (values: 0, 0.01, 0.02)
        *   Y-axis (left): Frequency (Hz) (values: 0, 500, 1000)
        *   X-axis: LComb (cm) (values: 68.5, 84, 100, 116)
    *   **Curves:**
        *   Baseline_H0 (oscillates mostly around 0 on P'RMS, 500 on Frequency)
        *   Baseline_H40 (oscillates mostly at a higher P'RMS than Baseline_H0
                        oscillates mostly around 500 on Frequency)

**3. Tables:**
*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 1: Experimental combustor rig**
    *   Components and Labels:
        *   Pilot Fuel
        *   Main Fuel
        *   Oxidizer Mixture
        *   Nozzle
        *   Quartz Liner
        *   Sampling Port
        *   Variable-length Section
        *   Exhaust

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Acoustic pressure fluctuation band: ±2.5 Hz
*   Camera filter bandwidth: 430±10 nm
*   Imaging frame rate: 2000 frames per second
*   Sampling probe downstream distance: 42 cm from dump plane, 39 cm ahead of cooled piston
*   Probe cooling water temperature: 70°C
*   Sample residence time in probe line: 1.4 s
*   Sample residence time in combustor before probe: 0.02 s
*   Window for averaging analog signals: 16s
*   Sweep of equivalence ratios: 0.7-1.00
*   Inlet temperature: 523 K
*   Pressure: 1 atm
*   Adiabatic flame temperature (Tad):  roughly 1834 K
*   Repeatability difference Measurements: less than 3% difference
*   Repeatability for CO and HC: 0.01% volume or 1.7% of the reading for CO and 3.3 ppm or 1.7% of the reading for HC

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Fig. 3: Comparison of CO2 measurements and equilibrium calculation values**
    *   **Axes:**
        *   X-axis: φ
        *   Y-axis: Vol %
    *   **Curves:**
        *   Analyzer CO2 Measurements
        *   Equilibrium CO2 - Wet basis
        *   Equilibrium CO2 - Dry basis

*   **Fig. 4: Comparison of calculated adiabatic flame temperatures from equilibrium calculations among five chemical kinetic models**
    *   **Axes:**
        *   X-axis: T\_ad calculated with USC mech II (K)
        *   Y-axis: Calculated T\_ad (K)
    *   **Curves:**
        *   GRI 3.0
        *   USC mech II
        *   Real Fuel 3
        *   ADTU
        *   FFCM-2

*   **Fig. 5: Comparison of calculated laminar flame speed (S°L) of the premixtures in the test matrix among five chemical kinetic models**
    *   **Axes:**
        *   X-axis: S°L calculated with USC mech II (cm/s)
        *   Y-axis: Calculated S°L (cm/s)
    *   **Curves:**
        *   GRI 3.0
        *   USC mech II
        *   Real Fuel 3
        *   ADTU
        *   FFCM-2

**3. Tables:**

*   Table 1 (Reference):Shows the test matrix. (Data not provided)

**4. Diagrams:**

*   Injector and combustor liner sketch in the top-left image of Fig 6. (no labels provided, not included in provided content)
    *   Components:Main flame, typical V-flame, a central pilot jet flame,

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Adiabatic flame temperature: 1834 K (Tad)
*   Standard deviation of adiabatic flame temperatures: 4 K (for four models excluding GRI 3.0)
*   Adiabatic flame temperature predicted by GRI 3.0: 1830 K
*   Pearson coefficients > 0.98 and > 0.99
*   Natural gas composition: 96.793% CH4, 3.05% C2H6, 0.139% nC3H8, 0.007% iC4H10, 0.011% nC4H10
*   Hydrogen content in the fuel varies from 0% to 40% by volume
*   O2 fraction varies from 21% to 15%
*   Wobbe Index range: 48-43 MJ/m³
*   Tad within 11 K of the baseline condition
*   The flame has a similar shape for the conditions with an inlet O₂ level above 17%.

[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 7]
Here's a summary of the technical content extracted from the provided text and image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   CO + OH = CO2 + H
*   H + O2 (+M) = HO2 (+M)
*   HO2 + OH = H2O + O2

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Fig. 8(a):**
    *   Axes: O2 (% vol), Test Condition (Baseline, C19, C17, N17, C15, N15)
    *   Curves: Bar chart grouped by H2 % (0, 10, 20, 30, 40)
    *   Key Values: Several values of O2 for the test conditions for each H2 concetrations.
*   **Fig. 8(b):**
    *   Axes: UHC (ppm), Test Condition (Baseline, C19, C17, N17, C15, N15)
    *   Curves: Bar chart grouped by test condition with error bars
*   **Fig. 9(a):**
    *   Axes: Combustion efficiency (%), Test Condition (Baseline, C19, C17, N17, C15, N15)
    *   Curves: Bar chart grouped by H2 % (0, 10, 20, 30, 40)
*   **Fig. 9(b):**
    *   Axes: CO2 (% vol), Coefficient of spatial uniformity
    *   Curves: Scattered plot for baseline, C15, C17, C19, N15, N17, colored by H2 content (0-40 %)
*   **Fig. 10 (Top):**
    *   Axes: Combustion efficiency, Flow residence time in PSR (s)
    *   Curves: Combustion efficiency (Baseline H40)
    *   Key Values: Residence time shift (0.01s), estimated residence time (0.02s)
*   **Fig. 10 (Bottom):**
    *   Axes: Mole fraction (ppm), Flow residence time in PSR (s)
    *   Curves: CH4 and CH2O mole fractions for Baseline H40.

**3. Tables:**

*   No Tables detected

**4. Diagrams:**

*   No Diagrams detected

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   H2 content: 0, 10, 20, 30, 40 %

*   Temperatures: above 1000 K

title: gtp-25-1250_EGR
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 8]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text and images:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   CO + OH = CO2 + H
*   H + O2 = O + OH
*   H2O + O = OH + OH
*   H2 + OH = H + H2O
*   CH2O + OH <=> H2O + HCO
*   CH4 + OH <=> CH3 + H2O

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*Fig. 11: Comparison between experimental and modeling results for CO2 (top), O2 (middle), and combustion efficiency (bottom)*

    *   **Top:** CO2 (% in dry exhaust) vs. Baseline, C19, C17, C15, N17, N15
        *   Curves: 0% H2, experiment; 10% H2, experiment; 20% H2, experiment; 30% H2, experiment; 40% H2 experiment; 0% H2, modeling; 10% H2, modeling; 20% H2, modeling; 30% H2, modeling; 40% H2, modeling
    *   **Middle:** O2 (% in dry exhaust) vs. Baseline, C19, C17, C15, N17, N15
        *   Curves: 0% H2, experiment; 10% H2, experiment; 20% H2, experiment; 30% H2, experiment; 40% H2 experiment; 0% H2, modeling; 10% H2, modeling; 20% H2, modeling; 30% H2, modeling; 40% H2, modeling
    *   **Bottom:** Combustion efficiency (%) vs. Baseline, C19, C17, C15, N17, N15
        *   Curves: 0% H2, experiment; 10% H2, experiment; 20% H2, experiment; 30% H2, experiment; 40% H2 experiment; 0% H2, modeling; 10% H2, modeling; 20% H2, modeling; 30% H2, modeling; 40% H2, modeling

*Fig. 12: Comparison of Damköhler number (Da) defined by the flow residence time matching experimentally determined combustion efficiency normalized by the flow residence time at extinction limit in PSR calculation*

    *   **Axes:**
        *   y axis: Da =τ/τextinction
        *   x axis: Baseline, C19, C17, C15, N17, N15
    *   **Curves:**  0% H2, modeling; 10% H2, modeling; 20% H2, modeling; 30% H2, modeling; 40% H2, modeling

*Fig. 13: Consumption pathways of OH radical at C15 and N15 cases at the flow residence time of 0.02 s in PSR calculations*

   *   **Axes:**
      *   y axis: (no label)
      *   x axis: 0, 0.05, 0.1, 0.15, 0.2, 0.25
   *   **Curves:** 40% H2, N15; 30% H2, N15; 20% H2, N15; 10% H2, N1
